This is a theological revision: by the time Chronicles was written (post-Exile), Jewish theology had developed the concept of Satan as a distinct figure, and the earlier account's portrayal of God provoking David to sin had become theologically embarrassing. The revision was made — creating an irreconcilable contradiction in scripture.

"शैतान इस्राएल के विरुद्ध खड़ा हुआ और उसने दाऊद को इस्राएल की गिनती करने के लिए उकसाया।" — वही घटना, अब शैतान को जिम्मेदार ठहराया गया। परमेश्वर को शैतान से बदल दिया गया।

ContradictionOld Testament

Does God Have a Physical Body? The Bible Says Bothक्या परमेश्वर का शरीर है? बाइबल दोनों कहती है

⚡ Internal Contradiction God's Physical Form — Direct Self-Contradiction

Christian theology holds that God is a pure spirit with no physical form. Yet the Old Testament repeatedly describes God with specific body parts — a face, hands, back, feet, mouth, nostrils — and describes people seeing God in person. John 4:24 says "God is a Spirit." Exodus 33:23 says God showed Moses his back. These are not metaphors in context — they are narrative descriptions of physical interactions.

ईसाई धर्मशास्त्र मानता है कि परमेश्वर शुद्ध आत्मा है। फिर भी पुराना नियम परमेश्वर के चेहरे, हाथ, पीठ, पैर और नथुनों का वर्णन करता है — और लोग उससे आमने-सामने मिलते हैं।

👁️
Exodus 33:20-23 · Genesis 32:30 · Exodus 24:9-11
निर्गमन ३३:२०-२३ · उत्पत्ति ३२:३० · निर्गमन २४:९-११
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

Exodus 33:20: God says "Thou canst not see my face: for there shall no man see me, and live." Exodus 33:23: God then shows Moses his back: "And I will take away mine hand, and thou shalt see my back parts: but my face shall not be seen." God has a back, a hand, and a face. Genesis 32:30: Jacob says "I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved." — Jacob saw God's face and survived — contradicting Exodus 33:20. Exodus 24:9-11: Moses, Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and seventy elders of Israel "saw the God of Israel: and there was under his feet as it were a paved work of a sapphire stone." God has feet and stands on sapphire pavement. John 4:24: "God is a Spirit." A spirit has no back, no hands, no feet, no face.

निर्गमन ३३:२३: परमेश्वर मूसा को अपनी पीठ दिखाता है। उत्पत्ति ३२:३०: याकूब कहता है "मैंने परमेश्वर को आमने-सामने देखा।" निर्गमन २४:१०: परमेश्वर के पैरों के नीचे नीलम पत्थर। यूहन्ना ४:२४: "परमेश्वर आत्मा है।" — आत्मा की पीठ, हाथ और पैर नहीं होते।

ContradictionOld TestamentNew Testament

Numbers 5: God's Own Prescribed Abortion Ritualगिनती ५: परमेश्वर का अपना गर्भपात अनुष्ठान

⚖️ Moral Failure God Prescribes an Abortifacient — In the Law

Numbers 5:11-31 contains God's own prescribed ritual — "the law of jealousy" — in which a priest gives a suspected adulteress a potion of water and dust from the tabernacle floor. If she is guilty, her "belly shall swell" and her "thigh shall rot" — causing her to miscarry. This is a divinely ordained abortifacient administered by a priest. The irony for modern anti-abortion Christians who cite the Bible: God himself prescribed a ritual to terminate pregnancy in his own law.

गिनती ५:११-३१ में परमेश्वर का अपना विधि-विधान है जिसमें एक पुजारी संदिग्ध व्यभिचारी स्त्री को एक औषधि देता है जो गर्भपात कराती है। परमेश्वर ने स्वयं अपने कानून में गर्भपात का नुस्खा लिखा।

🧪
Numbers 5:11-28 — The Law of Jealousy
गिनती ५:११-२८ — ईर्ष्या का नियम
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

Numbers 5:18-22: The priest takes "holy water" mixed with dust from the tabernacle floor and makes the woman drink it after a ritual curse: "And when he hath made her to drink the water, then it shall come to pass, that, if she be defiled, and have done trespass against her husband, that the water that causeth the curse shall enter into her, and become bitter, and her belly shall swell, and her thigh shall rot: and the woman shall be a curse among her people." The "thigh shall rot" (Hebrew: yarek nafal — literally "fallen thigh") is uniformly understood by Hebrew scholars as a reference to reproductive organ failure causing miscarriage or sterility. God's own law includes a priest-administered pregnancy-ending potion. The ritual is commanded by God (v.11: "The Lord spake unto Moses").

गिनती ५:२१-२२: "वह पानी जो श्राप लाता है उसके भीतर जाए और कड़वा हो जाए, और उसका पेट फूल जाए और उसकी जाँघ सड़ जाए।" — यह परमेश्वर का अपना नियम है (व.११: "यहोवा ने मूसा से कहा")। पुरोहित द्वारा दी गई गर्भपात-कारी औषधि।

Moral FailureOld Testament

Hell Is a New Testament Invention — The OT God Never Mentioned Itनरक नया नियम की खोज है — पुराने नियम के परमेश्वर ने कभी उल्लेख नहीं किया

📜 Historical Blunder The Bible's Greatest Theological Invention

If eternal conscious torment in hell is the ultimate consequence of sin — the most important fact any human could know — why does God never mention it in the first three-quarters of the Bible? The Old Testament uses Sheol — a shadowy underworld where all the dead go, good and evil alike, with no punishment. The concept of a fiery place of eternal torment only appears in the New Testament, largely in the teachings of Jesus. God gave the Israelites hundreds of laws, warnings, and punishments — but never once threatened them with eternal fire.

यदि नरक में अनंत यातना सबसे बड़ा परिणाम है, तो परमेश्वर ने बाइबल के पहले तीन-चौथाई भाग में इसका उल्लेख क्यों नहीं किया? पुराने नियम में केवल "शेओल" है — एक छायामय अधोलोक जहाँ सब मृत जाते हैं। अग्नि नरक केवल नए नियम में प्रकट होता है।

💀
Ecclesiastes 9:5 · Psalm 6:5 · Ezekiel 18:20
सभोपदेशक ९:५ · भजन ६:५ · यहेजकेल १८:२०
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

Ecclesiastes 9:5: "For the living know that they shall die: but the dead know not any thing, neither have they any more a reward; for the memory of them is forgotten." — The dead are unconscious. No hell, no heaven, no awareness. Psalm 6:5: "For in death there is no remembrance of thee: in the grave who shall give thee thanks?" — Death ends conscious existence. The God of Israel threatened his people with disease, drought, military defeat, childlessness, and exile for disobedience — across hundreds of chapters and dozens of prophets — and never once threatened eternal fire. Hell was imported into Christianity primarily from Greek thought (Tartarus, Hades) and later Near Eastern mythology — then read backwards into the Old Testament.

सभोपदेशक ९:५: "मृतक कुछ भी नहीं जानते।" भजन ६:५: "मृत्यु में तेरी कोई स्मृति नहीं।" — परमेश्वर ने इस्राएलियों को सूखा, बीमारी और युद्ध की धमकी दी — अनंत अग्नि की कभी नहीं। नरक यूनानी विचार से आयात किया गया।

Historical BlunderOld Testament
 🔥
Matthew 25:41 — Hell Appears Only in the NT
मत्ती २५:४१ — नरक केवल नए नियम में
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

Matthew 25:41: "Then shall he say also unto them on the left hand, Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels." Jesus introduces everlasting fire as a destination. Revelation 20:10: "And the devil that deceived them was cast into the lake of fire and brimstone, where the beast and the false prophet are, and shall be tormented day and night for ever and ever." — Eternal torment is a New Testament development. The theological inconsistency: the God who gave 613 commandments to Israel, who destroyed nations for idolatry, who flooded the earth — never once mentioned that disobedience would lead to eternal fire. He mentioned it precisely zero times. Jesus, supposedly the same God, makes it the centerpiece of his warnings.

मत्ती २५:४१: "शैतान और उसके दूतों के लिए तैयार अनंत अग्नि में जाओ।" — अनंत यातना का यह विचार नए नियम का विकास है। परमेश्वर ने ६१३ आज्ञाएं दीं लेकिन एक बार भी अनंत आग का उल्लेख नहीं किया।

Historical BlunderNew TestamentOld Testament

Jesus's "Sign of Jonah" Prophecy — Fails on Its Own Termsयीशु की "योना का चिन्ह" भविष्यवाणी — अपनी शर्तों पर विफल

⚡ Internal Contradiction Jesus's Own Prophecy Contradicts the Resurrection Timeline

Jesus gives one sign to verify his messiahship: the Sign of Jonah — three days and three nights in the earth, as Jonah was three days and three nights in the fish. The problem: the standard Christian timeline has Jesus dying on Good Friday afternoon and rising on Easter Sunday morning. That is one full day, two nights, and part of a day — not "three days and three nights" by any counting method. Jesus's own signature prophecy, used to validate everything, fails its own stated terms.

यीशु एक ही चिन्ह देता है: "योना का चिन्ह" — तीन दिन और तीन रात। मानक ईसाई मान्यता: शुक्रवार दोपहर मृत्यु, रविवार सुबह पुनरुत्थान। यह तीन दिन और तीन रात नहीं बनता। यीशु की अपनी भविष्यवाणी अपनी शर्तों पर असफल।

🐋
Matthew 12:38-40 · Jonah 1:17
मत्ती १२:३८-४० · योना १:१७
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

Matthew 12:39-40: "An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas: For as Jonas was three days and three nights in the whale's belly; so shall the Son of man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth." — Exact: three days AND three nights. Jonah 1:17: "And Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights." The comparison is literal. The problem: Matthew 27:46 — Jesus dies at the ninth hour (3pm) on the day of Preparation (Friday). Matthew 28:1 — He rises "as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week" (Sunday morning). Friday afternoon to Sunday morning = one day + parts of two others = two nights maximum. No counting method yields "three days and three nights." Jesus's singular validating sign does not fit the timeline his own gospel records.

मत्ती १२:४०: "योना जैसे तीन दिन और तीन रात मछली के पेट में रहा, वैसे ही मनुष्य का पुत्र तीन दिन और तीन रात पृथ्वी के भीतर रहेगा।" शुक्रवार दोपहर से रविवार सुबह = अधिकतम दो रात। यह तीन रात नहीं है।

ContradictionNew TestamentOld Testament

Hosea 1: God Commands a Prophet to Marry a Prostituteहोशे १: परमेश्वर एक नबी को वेश्या से विवाह करने का आदेश देता है

⚖️ Moral Failure God Commands What His Own Law Condemns

God commands the prophet Hosea to marry a woman of harlotry as a living metaphor for Israel's unfaithfulness. Leviticus 21:14 forbids priests from marrying harlots. God's own law for his holy men explicitly forbids this. God then commands Hosea to do exactly what the Law condemns, as a religious act of prophecy. Hosea 3 then instructs him to buy back his wife who has been engaging in adultery — an act God uses to illustrate his love for Israel. The metaphor requires ongoing adultery to work.

परमेश्वर होशे नबी को व्यभिचारिणी से विवाह करने का आदेश देता है। लेवीय २१:१४ पवित्र पुरुषों के लिए इसे स्पष्ट रूप से मना करता है। परमेश्वर अपने ही कानून का उल्लंघन अपने नबी को करवाता है।

💍
Hosea 1:2 · Hosea 3:1 · Leviticus 21:14
होशे १:२ · होशे ३:१ · लेवीय २१:१४
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

Hosea 1:2: "The Lord said to Hosea, Go, take unto thee a wife of whoredoms and children of whoredoms: for the land hath committed great whoredom, departing from the Lord." — God directly commands a prophet to marry a prostitute as a religious act. Hosea 3:1: God again commands: "Go yet, love a woman beloved of her friend, yet an adulteress, according to the love of the Lord toward the children of Israel." — God uses ongoing adultery as a positive metaphor for divine love. Leviticus 21:14: "A widow, or a divorced woman, or profane, or an harlot, these shall he not take: but he shall take a virgin of his own people to wife." — God's own law for his holy men explicitly forbids marrying a harlot. God commands Hosea to break God's own law as an act of religious devotion.

होशे १:२: "यहोवा ने होशे से कहा, जा, व्यभिचारिणी स्त्री को ब्याह ले।" लेवीय २१:१४: "वेश्या से विवाह न करे।" — परमेश्वर अपने नबी को अपना ही कानून तोड़ने का आदेश देता है।

ContradictionMoral FailureOld Testament

"Thou Shalt Not Suffer a Witch to Live" — God's Law"जादूगरनी को जीवित न रहने देना" — परमेश्वर का कानून

⚖️ Moral Failure Biblical Law That Killed Hundreds of Thousands

Exodus 22:18 is three words in Hebrew. It was taken literally by Christian authorities for over a thousand years. Conservative estimates place the number of people — overwhelmingly women — executed for witchcraft across Christian Europe and colonial America at 40,000–60,000, with broader estimates reaching 100,000. Every single execution was justified by direct citation of this verse as God's law. The text is unambiguous; the historical consequences are documented.

निर्गमन २२:१८ को ईसाई अधिकारियों ने एक हजार वर्षों से अधिक समय तक शाब्दिक रूप से लिया। रूढ़िवादी अनुमान: यूरोप और औपनिवेशिक अमेरिका में जादू-टोने के लिए ४०,०००-६०,००० लोगों को फाँसी दी गई — इस एक आयत को परमेश्वर के कानून के रूप में उद्धृत करते हुए।

🔥
Exodus 22:18 · Leviticus 20:27 · 1 Samuel 28
निर्गमन २२:१८ · लेवीय २०:२७ · १ शमूएल २८
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

Exodus 22:18: "Thou shalt not suffer a witch to live." — Three words. God's law. No qualifications, no context limits, no appeal process. Leviticus 20:27: "A man also or woman that hath a familiar spirit, or that is a wizard, shall surely be put to death: they shall stone them with stones." The irony of 1 Samuel 28: Saul consults the Witch of Endor — who successfully summons Samuel's spirit. God's own text confirms witchcraft works (the spirit of Samuel actually appears and speaks), while God's own law demands death for practicing it. Either witchcraft works and God's law kills people for a real skill, or witchcraft doesn't work and the Bible falsely confirms it does. Both positions damn the text.

निर्गमन २२:१८: "जादूगरनी को जीवित न रहने देना।" १ शमूएल २८: शाऊल एन्दोर की जादूगरनी से शमूएल की आत्मा बुलवाता है — और वह वास्तव में प्रकट होती है। बाइबल स्वयं पुष्टि करती है कि जादू काम करता है, जबकि परमेश्वर का कानून इसके लिए मृत्युदंड देता है।

Moral FailureOld Testament

Adam & Eve's Children — God's Plan Requires Incestआदम और हव्वा के बच्चे — परमेश्वर की योजना के लिए अनाचार आवश्यक

🔬 Scientific Error Genesis Requires Incest to Populate the Earth

Genesis presents Adam and Eve as the literal first two humans, ancestors of all humanity. Genesis 4 then names only three children: Cain, Abel, and Seth. Cain kills Abel, goes to "the land of Nod," finds a wife, and builds a city. Where did his wife come from? Where did the people of Nod come from? If Adam and Eve are the only humans, Cain's wife must be his sister. The human population of earth could only have grown through sibling incest — which God's own law (Leviticus 18:9) later explicitly condemns as an abomination. Additionally, a founding population of two humans is a genetic impossibility — modern genetics requires a minimum viable population of several thousand for a species to survive without fatal inbreeding collapse.

उत्पत्ति आदम और हव्वा को पहले दो मनुष्य बताता है। कैन "नोद की भूमि" में जाता है और एक पत्नी पाता है। यदि केवल आदम और हव्वा के बच्चे थे, तो कैन की पत्नी उसकी बहन थी। मानव जनसंख्या के विकास के लिए सहोदर अनाचार आवश्यक — जिसे बाद में परमेश्वर का कानून "घृणित" कहता है।

🧬
Genesis 4:16-17 · Genesis 5:4 · Leviticus 18:9
उत्पत्ति ४:१६-१७ · उत्पत्ति ५:४ · लेवीय १८:९
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

Genesis 4:16-17: "And Cain went out from the presence of the Lord, and dwelt in the land of Nod... And Cain knew his wife; and she conceived." — No origin given for the wife. No other humans have been created. Genesis 5:4: "And the days of Adam after he had begotten Seth were eight hundred years: and he begat sons and daughters." — Adam had other daughters, meaning Cain's wife was necessarily his sister or niece. Leviticus 18:9: "The nakedness of thy sister... thou shalt not uncover." — God's own law calls sibling incest shameful, punishable by death (Leviticus 20:17). The only way to populate the earth from two people is through incest — which God later forbids. Beyond theology: modern population genetics shows a founding population of two individuals would produce a genetic bottleneck causing catastrophic inbreeding within generations, making the entire species unviable.

उत्पत्ति ४:१७: कैन ने एक पत्नी को "जाना" — कोई मूल नहीं बताया। उत्पत्ति ५:४: आदम की बेटियाँ भी थीं — कैन की पत्नी उसकी बहन थी। लेवीय १८:९: सहोदर अनाचार "घृणित" और मृत्युदंड योग्य। आधुनिक जेनेटिक्स: दो व्यक्तियों से मानव जाति असंभव।

Scientific ErrorContradictionOld Testament

The Bible's Cosmology: Pillars, Windows, and a Solid Skyबाइबल का ब्रह्माण्डशास्त्र: खंभे, खिड़कियाँ और ठोस आकाश

🔬 Scientific Error Ancient Flat-Earth Cosmology as Divine Revelation

Beyond the firmament, the Bible's cosmological picture is consistent and consistently wrong: heaven has literal windows that open to release rain; the earth has literal foundations and pillars; the sky is a solid beaten metal dome. These are not poetic metaphors — they are functional descriptions used in narratives. The "windows of heaven" open for Noah's flood; the "pillars of the earth" shake when God is angry. The authors described the physical universe as they believed it literally was.

बाइबल का ब्रह्माण्डशास्त्र सुसंगत और सुसंगत रूप से गलत है: स्वर्ग में शाब्दिक खिड़कियाँ हैं जो बारिश छोड़ती हैं; पृथ्वी के खंभे हैं; आकाश ठोस पीटी हुई धातु का गुंबद है।

🏛️
Job 26:11 · 1 Samuel 2:8 · Genesis 7:11 · Job 38:22
अय्यूब २६:११ · १ शमूएल २:८ · उत्पत्ति ७:११ · अय्यूब ३८:२२
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

Job 26:11: "The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at his reproof." — Heaven has literal structural pillars. 1 Samuel 2:8: "For the pillars of the earth are the Lord's, and he hath set the world upon them." — Earth has literal pillars it rests on. Genesis 7:11: "The same day were all the fountains of the great deep broken up, and the windows of heaven were opened." — Heaven has literal windows that opened for Noah's flood. Job 38:22: "Hast thou entered into the treasuries of the snow? or hast thou seen the treasuries of the hail?" — Snow and hail are stored in heavenly warehouses. Psalm 75:3: "The earth and all the inhabitants thereof are dissolved: I bear up the pillars of it." God holds up the earth's pillars. None of this is poetic licence in context — it is functional cosmological description used to explain physical phenomena.

अय्यूब २६:११: "स्वर्ग के खंभे काँपते हैं।" १ शमूएल २:८: "पृथ्वी के खंभे यहोवा के हैं।" उत्पत्ति ७:११: "स्वर्ग की खिड़कियाँ खुल गईं।" अय्यूब ३८:२२: "क्या तू हिम के भण्डार में गया है?" — ब्रह्माण्ड की संरचना पूरी तरह गलत।

Scientific ErrorOld Testament
 🌍
Psalm 93:1 · Job 9:6 · Isaiah 11:12 — Immovable Earth
भजन ९३:१ · अय्यूब ९:६ · यशायाह ११:१२ — स्थिर पृथ्वी
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

Psalm 93:1: "The world also is stablished, that it cannot be moved." 1 Chronicles 16:30: "Fear before him, all the earth: the world also shall be stable, that it be not moved." Job 9:6: "Which shaketh the earth out of her place, and the pillars thereof tremble." Isaiah 11:12: God "shall gather together the dispersed of Judah from the four corners of the earth." Revelation 7:1: "I saw four angels standing on the four corners of the earth." — The earth is stationary, on pillars, with four corners. The earth orbits the sun at 67,000 mph, rotates at 1,000 mph, has no corners, and has no pillars. Every single cosmological claim the Bible makes about the physical structure of the earth is wrong.

भजन ९३:१: "पृथ्वी हिल नहीं सकती।" यशायाह ११:१२: "पृथ्वी के चारों कोनों से।" प्रकाशितवाक्य ७:१: "पृथ्वी के चारों कोनों पर।" — पृथ्वी ६७,००० मील प्रति घंटे से सूर्य की परिक्रमा करती है, घूमती है, और न खंभे हैं न कोने। बाइबल की हर ब्रह्माण्डशास्त्रीय घोषणा गलत है।

Scientific ErrorOld TestamentNew Testament

Infinite Torture for Finite Crimes — The Doctrine of Eternal Hellसीमित अपराधों के लिए अनंत यातना — नरक का सिद्धांत

⚖️ Moral Failure Central Christian Doctrine — Morally Bankrupt

The New Testament — specifically the words attributed to Jesus — introduces the doctrine of eternal conscious torment in fire for anyone who does not believe. No finite crime can morally warrant infinite punishment. A God who tortures souls without end for disbelief during a single human lifetime is, by any rational ethical framework, not a moral being. Jesus speaks of hell more than any other New Testament figure — this is not a peripheral teaching.

नया नियम — विशेष रूप से यीशु के शब्द — अविश्वास के लिए अनन्त आग में जलने का सिद्धांत प्रस्तुत करता है। कोई भी सीमित अपराध नैतिक रूप से असीमित दंड का औचित्य नहीं दे सकता। यीशु नरक का किसी भी अन्य व्यक्ति से अधिक उल्लेख करता है।

🔥
Matthew 25:41, 46
मत्ती २५:४१, ४६
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

Jesus: "Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels." And: "These shall go away into everlasting punishment." The Greek word is aiónios — the same word used for eternal life. Jesus is unambiguous: the punishment is without end. A being who creates humans knowing they will sin, then sentences them to literally endless agony, cannot be called good by any coherent ethical definition.

यीशु: "हे शापित लोगो, मेरे पास से चले जाओ, उस अनन्त आग में जो शैतान और उसके दूतों के लिए तैयार की गई है।" यूनानी शब्द aiónios वही है जो अनन्त जीवन के लिए उपयोग होता है — दंड भी उतना ही अनन्त है।

Direct quoteNew TestamentTheological Contradiction
 😰
Revelation 14:10-11
प्रकाशितवाक्य १४:१०-११
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

"The same shall drink of the wine of the wrath of God... and he shall be tormented with fire and brimstone in the presence of the holy angels, and in the presence of the Lamb: And the smoke of their torment ascendeth up for ever and ever: and they have no rest day nor night." The torture is explicitly described as unceasing — no rest, no end, in the presence of Jesus and the angels who watch. This is the Bible's most explicit statement on eternal conscious torment.

"उनकी पीड़ा का धुआँ युगानुयुग उठता रहेगा और दिन-रात उन्हें चैन नहीं।" यह बाइबल का अनन्त सचेत यातना का सबसे स्पष्ट वर्णन है — यीशु और स्वर्गदूतों की उपस्थिति में।

Direct quoteNew TestamentMoral Failure

God Commands: Kill Anyone Who Tries to Lead You Away from the Faithपरमेश्वर का आदेश: जो विश्वास से दूर ले जाए उसे मार डालो

⚖️ Moral Failure Death Penalty for Other Religions

Deuteronomy 13 — the law Moses delivered from God — commands that if anyone, including your own family members, tries to lead you to worship other gods, you must kill them personally. Your hand must be first. There is no trial, no grace period, no option for the person to recant. Your own brother, your son, your daughter, the wife of your bosom — if they suggest exploring another religion, God commands you to execute them yourself. This passage was used to justify Christian Inquisitions and the execution of heretics for over a thousand years.

व्यवस्थाविवरण १३ — परमेश्वर का कानून: यदि आपका कोई परिवार का सदस्य आपको दूसरे देवताओं की पूजा के लिए प्रेरित करे तो उसे खुद मार डालो। आपका हाथ पहले उठे। इस श्लोक का उपयोग ईसाई धर्माधिकरण और विधर्मियों की हत्या को उचित ठहराने के लिए किया गया।

🗡️
Deuteronomy 13:6-10
व्यवस्थाविवरण १३:६-१०
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

"If thy brother, the son of thy mother, or thy son, or thy daughter, or the wife of thy bosom, or thy friend... entice thee secretly, saying, Let us go and serve other gods... thou shalt surely kill him; thine hand shall be first upon him to put him to death, and afterwards the hand of all the people. And thou shalt stone him with stones, that he die." This is God speaking directly through Moses. Your closest relatives — if they express religious curiosity — must be executed by your hand first, then by mob stoning.

"यदि तेरा भाई, तेरा पुत्र, तेरी पुत्री, तेरी पत्नी... तुझे छिपकर फुसलाए... तू उसे अवश्य मार डाल; उस पर पहले तेरा हाथ उठे।" परमेश्वर का प्रत्यक्ष आदेश: धार्मिक जिज्ञासा रखने वाले परिवार के सदस्य को पत्थर मारकर मार डालो।

Direct quoteOld TestamentMoral Failure

God Punishes David by Having His Wives Raped in Publicपरमेश्वर दाऊद को दंड देने के लिए उसकी पत्नियों का सार्वजनिक बलात्कार करवाता है

⚖️ Moral Failure God Uses Rape of Innocents as Judicial Punishment

God punishes David for the Bathsheba affair not by punishing David directly — but by having David's own wives gang-raped in public by his son Absalom, in broad daylight, on the palace roof. God announces this in advance as His deliberate punishment. The wives are entirely innocent. They are punished sexually, publicly, and in broad daylight specifically because God chose this as the punishment instrument. The Bible records this without condemnation of God's method.

परमेश्वर दाऊद को दंड देता है — बाथशेबा मामले के लिए — दाऊद की पत्नियों को उसके पुत्र अबशालोम द्वारा दिन-दहाड़े महल की छत पर सार्वजनिक रूप से बलात्कार करवाकर। पत्नियाँ पूरी तरह निर्दोष हैं। परमेश्वर इसे अपना न्यायिक दंड घोषित करता है।

👁️
2 Samuel 12:11-12
२ शमूएल १२:११-१२
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

God speaks through Nathan: "Thus saith the LORD, Behold, I will raise up evil against thee out of thine own house, and I will take thy wives before thine eyes, and give them unto thy neighbour, and he shall lie with thy wives in the sight of this sun. For thou didst it secretly: but I will do this thing before all Israel, and before the sun." God is the explicit architect of the mass rape of innocent women as a punishment instrument. Their suffering is the point.

परमेश्वर नाथान के द्वारा कहता है: "मैं तेरी पत्नियों को तेरे सामने ले जाऊँगा और उन्हें तेरे पड़ोसी को दे दूँगा, वह इस सूर्य के सामने उनके साथ सोएगा।" परमेश्वर स्वयं निर्दोष स्त्रियों के सामूहिक बलात्कार का वास्तुकार है।

Direct quoteOld TestamentMoral Failure

God Kills 70,000 Innocent People to Punish David's Censusपरमेश्वर दाऊद की जनगणना के कारण ७०,००० निर्दोष लोगों को मारता है

⚖️ Moral Failure Mass Collective Punishment of the Innocent

David conducts a census — which God (or Satan, depending on which book you read) incited. God then gives David three punishment options. David chooses a plague. God sends a plague that kills 70,000 Israelites — people who had nothing to do with the census. God then stops the plague not because he is merciful, but because he sees the angel killing and arbitrarily decides it is "enough." The 70,000 dead are simply collateral in a dispute between God and a king.

दाऊद जनगणना करता है। परमेश्वर तीन दंड विकल्प देता है। दाऊद महामारी चुनता है। परमेश्वर ७०,००० इस्राएलियों को मार देता है — जो जनगणना से सर्वथा असंबंधित थे। यह परमेश्वर और एक राजा के बीच विवाद में ७०,००० निर्दोषों की मृत्यु है।

💀
2 Samuel 24:10-15
२ शमूएल २४:१०-१५
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

God offers David three punishments: seven years of famine, three months of flight from enemies, or three days of pestilence. David says "let us fall now into the hand of the LORD." God sends plague: "So the LORD sent a pestilence upon Israel from the morning even to the time appointed: and there died of the people from Dan even to Beersheba seventy thousand men." David sinned. 70,000 uninvolved Israelites die. This is the God of love administering divine justice.

परमेश्वर ने इस्राएल पर महामारी भेजी: "दान से लेकर बेर्शेबा तक सत्तर हजार पुरुष मर गए।" दाऊद ने पाप किया — ७०,००० निर्दोष मरे। यही प्रेम के परमेश्वर का न्याय है।

Direct quoteOld TestamentMoral Failure

Amnon Rapes His Sister Tamar — God Does Nothing, David Does Nothingअम्नोन ने तामार का बलात्कार किया — परमेश्वर कुछ नहीं करता, दाऊद कुछ नहीं करता

⚖️ Moral Failure Rape of a Royal Daughter — No Divine Condemnation

David's son Amnon becomes obsessed with his half-sister Tamar, tricks her into his bedroom, and rapes her. Tamar pleads with him beforehand. After the rape, he despises her and throws her out. Tamar is left desolate in her brother Absalom's house. The text records David's reaction: he was "very wroth" — and did absolutely nothing. God issues no condemnation, sends no punishment for Amnon, performs no miracle of justice. Amnon is eventually killed by Absalom — not by God, not by law, not by David.

दाऊद के पुत्र अम्नोन ने अपनी सौतेली बहन तामार का बलात्कार किया। दाऊद "बहुत क्रोधित" हुआ — और कुछ नहीं किया। परमेश्वर ने कोई निंदा नहीं की, कोई दंड नहीं दिया। अम्नोन को अंततः अबशालोम ने मारा — परमेश्वर ने नहीं।

😔
2 Samuel 13:1-22
२ शमूएल १३:१-२२
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

Tamar begs: "Nay, my brother, do not force me; for no such thing ought to be done in Israel." Amnon overpowers and rapes her. After: "Then Amnon hated her exceedingly... Amnon said unto her, Arise, be gone." Tamar remains "desolate." David "heard of all these things, and he was very wroth" — then the text records no action, no punishment, no legal consequence, no divine intervention. God, who killed 70,000 for a census, does nothing for Tamar. She is one of the Bible's most discarded victims.

तामार ने विनती की: "नहीं भाई, मुझे बलात्कृत मत करो।" अम्नोन ने उसे बलात्कृत किया। दाऊद "बहुत क्रोधित हुआ" — और कुछ नहीं किया। परमेश्वर ने जनगणना के लिए ७०,००० को मारा, तामार के लिए कुछ नहीं किया।

Direct quoteOld TestamentMoral Failure

God Murders Every Firstborn Child in Egypt — Including Slaves and Animalsपरमेश्वर मिस्र में हर पहलौठे बच्चे को मारता है — दासों और पशुओं सहित

⚖️ Moral Failure Mass Execution of Innocent Children as Divine Justice

The tenth plague of Exodus is God personally killing every firstborn in Egypt — from Pharaoh's son to the firstborn of the slave girl behind the mill, to the firstborn of cattle. God explicitly admits He hardened Pharaoh's heart Himself (Exodus 4:21, 10:1) to prevent Pharaoh from letting the Israelites go — specifically so God could demonstrate His power through escalating plagues. The children of Egyptian slaves, who had no authority over Pharaoh and were themselves oppressed, are killed as collective punishment for their ruler's God-manipulated stubbornness.

परमेश्वर ने स्वयं फ़िरौन का मन कठोर किया (निर्गमन ४:२१) ताकि वह इस्राएलियों को न जाने दे — फिर दंड के रूप में मिस्र के हर पहलौठे को मार डाला, जिसमें दासों के बच्चे और पशु भी शामिल थे।

💀
Exodus 12:29-30 · Exodus 4:21
निर्गमन १२:२९-३० · निर्गमन ४:२१
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

Exodus 12:29: "The LORD smote all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh... unto the firstborn of the captive that was in the dungeon; and all the firstborn of cattle." Exodus 4:21: God tells Moses beforehand: "I will harden his heart, that he shall not let the people go." God deliberately prevents Pharaoh from complying, then kills children for Pharaoh's God-induced stubbornness. The captive's firstborn child — enslaved, powerless, with zero connection to Pharaoh's decisions — is executed as divine collateral.

निर्गमन १२:२९: "यहोवा ने मिस्र में सभी पहलौठों को मारा — फ़िरौन के पहलौठे से लेकर कैदी के पहलौठे तक और पशुओं के पहलौठे तक।" निर्गमन ४:२१: "मैं उसका मन कठोर करूँगा।" परमेश्वर ने स्वयं फ़िरौन को रोका, फिर निर्दोष बच्चों को मार डाला।

Direct quoteOld TestamentMoral Failure

A Man Gathers Sticks on the Sabbath — God Orders His Execution by Stoningएक व्यक्ति सब्त के दिन लकड़ियाँ इकट्ठा करता है — परमेश्वर उसे पत्थर मारकर मारने का आदेश देता है

⚖️ Moral Failure Capital Punishment for Basic Labor

A man is found gathering sticks on the Sabbath. He is arrested and held in custody while Moses asks God what to do. God's answer: stone him to death. The whole congregation takes him outside the camp and stones him until he is dead. This is not a metaphor. This is a named divine legal ruling. The man's name is never given. His family is never mentioned. He picks up sticks and God personally sentences him to community stoning. This passage has no equivalent anywhere in ancient Near Eastern law codes.

एक व्यक्ति सब्त के दिन लकड़ियाँ इकट्ठा करता पाया गया। मूसा ने परमेश्वर से पूछा क्या करें। परमेश्वर का उत्तर: उसे पत्थर मारकर मार डालो। सारी मंडली ने उसे बाहर ले जाकर मार डाला। लकड़ी उठाने के लिए मृत्युदंड।

🪵
Numbers 15:32-36
गिनती १५:३२-३६
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

"And while the children of Israel were in the wilderness, they found a man that gathered sticks upon the sabbath day... And the LORD said unto Moses, The man shall be surely put to death: all the congregation shall stone him with stones without the camp. And all the congregation brought him without the camp, and stoned him with stones, and he died." This verdict comes directly from God. No trial, no defence, no mercy, no context for why he gathered sticks — poverty, cold, hunger — none of it matters. God's answer to sabbath stick-gathering is mandatory community execution.

"यहोवा ने मूसा से कहा, वह मनुष्य अवश्य मारा जाए; सारी मंडली उसे डेरे के बाहर पत्थर मारे।" मंडली ने उसे बाहर ले जाकर मार डाला। लकड़ी उठाने के लिए परमेश्वर का प्रत्यक्ष मृत्युदंड।

Direct quoteOld TestamentMoral Failure

Elijah Wins a Contest Then Massacres 450 Prisoners — God Rewards Himएलिय्याह प्रतियोगिता जीतता है फिर ४५० बंदियों का नरसंहार करता है — परमेश्वर उसे पुरस्कृत करता है

⚖️ Moral Failure Mass Execution of Prisoners of Faith

Elijah challenges the prophets of Baal to a contest on Mount Carmel. God sends fire; Baal does not respond. Elijah wins. His next act: "Take the prophets of Baal; let not one of them escape." He takes all 450 men — who have done nothing violent — to the brook Kishon and slaughters every one of them. God's response to this mass execution of prisoners: He rewards Elijah with a miraculous chariot ride to heaven (2 Kings 2). No condemnation, no rebuke, no consequence — only divine reward.

एलिय्याह ने बाल के ४५० नबियों को नदी किशोन पर ले जाकर मार डाला। ये लोग कैदी थे, निहत्थे थे। परमेश्वर की प्रतिक्रिया: एलिय्याह को स्वर्ग ले जाने के लिए एक चमत्कारी रथ। कोई निंदा नहीं।

🗡️
1 Kings 18:40
१ राजा १८:४०
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

"And Elijah said unto them, Take the prophets of Baal; let not one of them escape. And they took them: and Elijah brought them down to the brook Kishon, and slew them there." 450 men. All killed. They were not combatants; they were prophets who had just participated in a public religious contest. After the contest they were captives. Elijah executes them en masse at a river. God then rewards Elijah: in 2 Kings 2, he is taken directly to heaven in a whirlwind. The massacre earns no divine rebuke whatsoever.

"एलिय्याह ने उन्हें किशोन नदी पर ले जाकर वहाँ मार डाला।" ४५० व्यक्ति — सब मारे गए। वे लड़ाके नहीं थे। एलिय्याह को तुरंत बाद स्वर्ग में रथ से ले जाया गया। कोई दंड नहीं।

Direct quoteOld TestamentMoral Failure

Psalm 137: "Happy Shall He Be That Dasheth Thy Little Ones Against the Stones"भजन संहिता १३७: "धन्य है वह जो तेरे बच्चों को पत्थर पर पटके"

⚖️ Moral Failure Glorification of Brutal Retribution

Psalm 137 is one of the most celebrated psalms in Christian tradition — set to music, quoted in literature, included in lectionaries. Its final verses are virtually never read aloud in churches. The psalmist, addressing Babylon, calls anyone "happy" who seizes Babylonian infants and smashes their heads against rocks. This is not a prophecy of what will happen. It is a wish, a blessing pronounced on whoever kills the babies. It is canonical, inspired scripture — presented in the Bible as devotional poetry.

भजन संहिता १३७ ईसाई परंपरा में सबसे प्रसिद्ध भजनों में से एक है। इसके अंतिम श्लोक चर्च में कभी नहीं पढ़े जाते। भजनकार बाबुल के शिशुओं को पत्थर पर पटकने वाले को "धन्य" कहता है। यह प्रेरित, विहित धर्मग्रंथ है — भक्ति काव्य के रूप में प्रस्तुत।

🪨
Psalm 137:8-9
भजन संहिता १३७:८-९
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

"O daughter of Babylon, who art to be destroyed; happy shall he be, that rewardeth thee as thou hast served us. Happy shall he be, that taketh and dasheth thy little ones against the stones." This is canonical scripture. It blesses — pronounces happiness upon — anyone who seizes Babylonian infants and smashes them against rocks. This is not a neutral description of war. It is a joyful blessing on the killer of babies, presented as devotional poetry inspired by God, included in the Psalter used for Christian worship for two thousand years.

"धन्य है वह जो तुझे बदला देगा... धन्य है वह जो तेरे बच्चों को पकड़कर पत्थर पर पटकेगा।" यह विहित धर्मग्रंथ है। यह शिशुओं को पत्थर पर पटकने वाले को धन्य कहता है — दो हजार वर्षों से ईसाई उपासना में शामिल।

Direct quoteOld TestamentMoral Failure

Dinah's Rape Leads to Deceptive Mass Slaughter of an Entire Cityदीना के बलात्कार के बाद धोखे से एक पूरे शहर का नरसंहार

⚖️ Moral Failure Collective Punishment — Entire City Massacred by Deception

Shechem rapes Dinah, then asks to marry her. Jacob's sons agree — on condition all the men of the city be circumcised. The men comply. On the third day, while all the men are still in pain and unable to fight, Simeon and Levi enter the city and kill every single male. They then take the women, children, cattle, and all goods as plunder. Jacob's only objection is not moral — it is strategic: "ye have troubled me to make me stink among the inhabitants of the land." God never condemns the massacre, the deception, or the mass enslavement.

याकूब के पुत्रों ने धोखे से सभी पुरुषों को खतना कराया, फिर तीसरे दिन दर्द में असहाय सभी पुरुषों को मार डाला और स्त्रियों व बच्चों को लूट लिया। याकूब की आपत्ति नैतिक नहीं — रणनीतिक थी। परमेश्वर ने कभी निंदा नहीं की।

🏙️
Genesis 34:1-29
उत्पत्ति ३४:१-२९
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

"And it came to pass on the third day, when they were sore, that two of the sons of Jacob, Simeon and Levi, Dinah's brethren, took each man his sword, and came upon the city boldly, and slew all the males." They then "took their sheep, and their oxen, and their asses... and their little ones, and their wives." Every man dead. Every woman and child enslaved. Every possession stolen. Their crime: the men accepted circumcision as a peace condition, then were slaughtered while incapacitated. Jacob's response is entirely about reputation. God's response: silence and approval — in Genesis 49, Simeon and Levi are cursed only for their anger, not their massacre.

"तीसरे दिन जब वे दर्द में थे, तब सिमोन और लेवी ने आकर सभी पुरुषों को मार डाला।" फिर स्त्रियों, बच्चों और सारी संपत्ति को लूट लिया। परमेश्वर का उत्तर: मौन। उत्पत्ति ४९ में उन्हें केवल उनके क्रोध के लिए डाँटा गया — नरसंहार के लिए नहीं।

Direct quoteOld TestamentMoral Failure

God Commands Abraham to Burn His Son Alive — Then Calls This a Test of Faithपरमेश्वर इब्राहीम को अपने पुत्र को जलाने का आदेश देता है — फिर इसे विश्वास की परीक्षा कहता है

⚖️ Moral Failure God Commands Child Sacrifice as a Virtue

God commands Abraham to take his son Isaac, travel three days, and offer him as a burnt offering. Abraham complies. He binds his son on an altar and raises the knife to kill him. God stops him at the last second and declares Abraham passed the test. This story is presented as the pinnacle of faith and obedience. The moral problem is foundational: a God who commands child murder — even as a test — is commanding something intrinsically evil. Abraham's willingness to kill his child is praised, not condemned. The child's terror goes unaddressed entirely.

परमेश्वर ने इब्राहीम को इसहाक को होमबलि चढ़ाने का आदेश दिया। इब्राहीम ने चाकू उठाया। परमेश्वर ने अंतिम क्षण रोका और कहा: तुमने परीक्षा पास की। यह विश्वास की पराकाष्ठा के रूप में प्रस्तुत है। बच्चे का भय का कोई उल्लेख नहीं।

🔪
Genesis 22:1-12
उत्पत्ति २२:१-१२
King James Versionकिंग जेम्स संस्करण

"Take now thy son, thine only son Isaac, whom thou lovest, and get thee into the land of Moriah; and offer him there for a burnt offering." Abraham travels three days, builds the altar, binds his son, and raises the knife. "And Abraham stretched forth his hand, and took the knife to slay his son." God stops him: "now I know that thou fearest God." The text presents Abraham's willingness to murder his child as a virtue — the supreme demonstration of faith. Hebrews 11:17-19 and James 2:21 praise Abraham for this. No scripture expresses concern for Isaac's experience of being bound, placed on wood, and nearly killed by his father on divine instruction.

"अपने पुत्र इसहाक को होमबलि के रूप में चढ़ाओ।" इब्राहीम ने यात्रा की, वेदी बनाई, पुत्र को बाँधा और चाकू उठाया। परमेश्वर ने रोका: "अब मैं जान गया।" इब्रानियों ११ और याकूब २ इसकी प्रशंसा करते हैं। इसहाक के भय का कोई उल्लेख नहीं।

Direct quoteOld TestamentMoral Failure
